skill.name: algorithmic-art
skill.description: Creates original generative art concepts and self-contained p5.js viewers with seeded randomness and interactive parameter controls.
skill.summary: Create reproducible p5.js generative art with interactive controls.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: The scanner reported many high and critical matches, but review found they are false positives from license text, markdown examples, regex usage, and comments. The only confirmed risk is low: generated browser artifacts load p5.js and fonts from public CDNs. No evidence found of credential access, shell command execution, weak cryptography use, obfuscation, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: External Browser Dependencies
low_findings.description: The viewer template loads p5.js and Google Fonts from public CDNs. This is a normal browser dependency for interactive art, but it creates a supply-chain and availability dependency. No evidence found that user data or secrets are sent to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external script and font URLs are directly present in the template. The surrounding code only loads public assets, so the risk is limited to dependency trust.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, shell execution, Python exec, Windows SAM, and dangerous-combination findings do not reflect executable threats. They match Apache license wording, markdown code fences, a JavaScript regular expression exec call, and comments. No evidence found of command execution, credential access, obfuscation, or malicious intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were reviewed in context and are documentation, template comments, or safe JavaScript APIs. I found no semantic evidence supporting the scanner's critical or high verdicts.

content.value_statement: Generative art requests often need clear concepts, reproducible seeds, and usable controls. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create interactive p5.js artworks.
actual_capabilities: Creates an algorithmic art philosophy before implementation.
Builds self-contained HTML viewers based on p5.js.
Uses seeded randomness for repeatable visual variations.
Adds interactive sliders, color controls, and seed navigation.
Supports particle systems, flow fields, recursion, and procedural geometry.
limitations: Generated artifacts depend on browser support for JavaScript and canvas.
The template loads p5.js and fonts from external CDNs.
The skill does not render final images by itself.
Complex sketches may need performance tuning on slower devices.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Generative Poster
use_cases.description: Create a reproducible p5.js artwork with sliders for visual exploration.

use_cases.title: Teach Creative Coding
use_cases.description: Explain a computational art concept and turn it into an interactive browser sketch.

use_cases.title: Explore Visual Systems
use_cases.description: Build seed-based variations for particles, flow fields, or procedural patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single interactive piece.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a calm p5.js generative artwork inspired by rain on glass. Include seeded randomness and three useful controls.

prompt_templates.title: Develop a Visual System
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with a concept and parameters.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an algorithmic art philosophy for coral growth, then build a self-contained p5.js viewer with seed controls and growth parameters.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Variations
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for multiple seed outcomes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an interactive flow-field artwork and identify five seeds with distinct visual moods. Explain what each seed changes.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Performance and Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize this generative art concept for smooth browser performance. Add precise controls for density, motion, palette, and export behavior.

output_examples.input: Create a generative artwork about magnetic memory.
output_examples.output: A short art philosophy, followed by an interactive p5.js viewer with particles, field strength controls, seed navigation, and color settings.

output_examples.input: Make a calm procedural piece for a website background.
output_examples.output: A browser-ready artwork with restrained motion, repeatable seeds, adjustable density, and notes about performance-friendly settings.

output_examples.input: Show 100 possible variations of a flow field.
output_examples.output: A seed-driven viewer that lets the user step through variations and select promising seeds for later refinement.

best_practices: Start with the philosophy, then choose algorithms that express it.
Keep parameter ranges small enough for smooth interaction.
Use seed values to save and revisit strong compositions.
anti_patterns: Do not copy a living artist's recognizable style.
Do not add unrelated controls that do not affect the artwork.
Do not rely on random colors without a clear palette strategy.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates original generative art concepts and interactive p5.js HTML viewers.

faq.question: Can the same artwork be reproduced later?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill uses seed values so the same settings can recreate the same output.

faq.question: Does it need a server?
faq.answer: No. The intended output is a single HTML artifact that opens in a browser.

faq.question: Does it use external resources?
faq.answer: Yes. The template loads p5.js and Google Fonts from public CDNs.

faq.question: Can users adjust the artwork?
faq.answer: Yes. The template supports sliders, color controls, seed navigation, reset, and regeneration.

faq.question: Is it safe for copyrighted styles?
faq.answer: The skill directs the model to create original work instead of copying existing artists.
